stage.name: Address translation: MMU, TLB, and PTW on the data path
stage.description: A virtual-memory lesson connecting MMUEn, Bare/Sv modes, TLB lookup, PTW miss handling, and load/store translation requests.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: A memory address may be a question, not an answer

content.title: Address translation: MMU, TLB, and PTW
content.subtitle: When virtual memory is enabled, the load/store address must be translated, cached, checked, and sometimes walked through page tables.
content.thesis: Translation is the step that turns a core-visible virtual address into a physical memory request or a precise exception.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 Programmer View: RISC-V virtual memory
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMUEn and XLEN; CV32A6 supports Sv32, CV64A6 supports Sv39, and Bare mode is the default.

content.speakerNotes: Up to now, we talked as if an address was already ready for memory. With virtual memory, it is not. The programmer view explains that CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMU configuration and addressing mode: CV32A6 can support Sv32, CV64A6 can support Sv39, and Bare mode is the default. The LSU has to live inside that world.

actions.text: Up to now, we talked as if an address was already ready for memory. With virtual memory, it is not. The programmer view explains that CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMU configuration and addressing mode: CV32A6 can support Sv32, CV64A6 can support Sv39, and Bare mode is the default. The LSU has to live inside that world.

scenes.title: Start from configuration, not from one universal MMU

content.title: CVA6 virtual memory is configuration-dependent
content.subtitle: Do not assume every CVA6 variant has the same address-translation behavior.

cards.title: MMUEn
cards.body: If MMUEn is disabled, the core remains in Bare mode and satp writes are ignored.

cards.title: CV32A6
cards.body: With MMUEn and 32-bit XLEN, Sv32 is the relevant virtual-memory mode.

cards.title: CV64A6
cards.body: With MMUEn and 64-bit XLEN, Sv39 is the relevant virtual-memory mode.

cards.title: CV32A60X caveat
cards.body: The documented CV32A60X profile integrates no MMU and supports Bare mode only.

content.takeaway: Before teaching TLBs, identify which CVA6 configuration you are discussing.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 Programmer View: RISC-V virtual memory
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMUEn and XLEN; CV32A6 supports Sv32, CV64A6 supports Sv39, and Bare mode is the default.

content.speakerNotes: This is exactly why Unit 02 mattered. CVA6 is a family, not a single frozen chip. Some configurations integrate an MMU; some do not. Bare mode is the default. So a professional course should not say CVA6 always uses Sv39, or always has a DTLB. It should say which profile and configuration are being discussed.

actions.text: This is exactly why Unit 02 mattered. CVA6 is a family, not a single frozen chip. Some configurations integrate an MMU; some do not. Bare mode is the default. So a professional course should not say CVA6 always uses Sv39, or always has a DTLB. It should say which profile and configuration are being discussed.

scenes.title: Translation path: fast hit or page walk

content.title: The MMU tries a cached translation before walking page tables
content.subtitle: A TLB hit is the fast path; a TLB miss invokes the PTW.

steps.title: Virtual address arrives
steps.body: Fetch or LSU asks for an address that may need translation.

steps.title: TLB lookup
steps.body: ITLB or DTLB checks whether the virtual-to-physical mapping is already cached.

steps.title: PTW on miss
steps.body: The page-table walker performs a multi-cycle walk and may update the TLB.

steps.title: Physical address or exception
steps.body: A successful walk produces translation; an error state becomes a page-fault exception.

content.takeaway: The MMU is a latency path and an exception path.

assets.caption: Official MMU block diagram showing translation-related structure.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 deprecated design docs: MMU and PTW
sourceAnchors.locator: deprecated-design-docs/ex_stage.md Memory Management Unit / Page Table Walker
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The MMU contains ITLB, DTLB, and PTW; the PTW listens for ITLB or DTLB misses and raises page faults on error states.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 Programmer View: RISC-V virtual memory
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMUEn and XLEN; CV32A6 supports Sv32, CV64A6 supports Sv39, and Bare mode is the default.

content.speakerNotes: The simplified flow is friendly enough. A virtual address arrives. The relevant TLB checks for a cached translation. If it hits, translation is fast. If it misses, the page-table walker performs a walk. If the walk succeeds, the TLB can be updated and the access continues. If the walk fails, the result is not data; it is an exception.

actions.text: The simplified flow is friendly enough. A virtual address arrives. The relevant TLB checks for a cached translation. If it hits, translation is fast. If it misses, the page-table walker performs a walk. If the walk succeeds, the TLB can be updated and the access continues. If the walk fails, the result is not data; it is an exception.

scenes.title: The LSU asks translation as part of memory execution

content.title: Load and store units request translation before memory side effects
content.subtitle: The data side is handshake-driven and tied to load/store legality.

columns.title: Load unit
columns.body: The load path sends a translation request, virtual address, and receives physical address or exception information.

columns.title: Store unit
columns.body: The store path also requests translation before a store can become a valid committed memory write.

content.takeaway: Translation is part of the LSU contract, not a separate classroom afterthought.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A65X design: load_unit
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The load unit checks the store buffer before issuing a load and stalls load-after-store cases when the older store has not reached the cache.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A65X design: store_unit
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Stores can be speculative, so outstanding stores are tracked separately from committed stores; commit turns an outstanding store into a committed one.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 deprecated design docs: MMU and PTW
sourceAnchors.locator: deprecated-design-docs/ex_stage.md Memory Management Unit / Page Table Walker
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The MMU contains ITLB, DTLB, and PTW; the PTW listens for ITLB or DTLB misses and raises page faults on error states.

content.speakerNotes: The data side matters because the LSU generates addresses and then asks the MMU or PMP path whether the access is legal and where it should go physically. The load unit and store unit both expose translation request and virtual-address signals in the design text. A bad translation can turn a memory operation into an exception before it becomes a real memory access.

actions.text: The data side matters because the LSU generates addresses and then asks the MMU or PMP path whether the access is legal and where it should go physically. The load unit and store unit both expose translation request and virtual-address signals in the design text. A bad translation can turn a memory operation into an exception before it becomes a real memory access.

scenes.title: TLB entries are performance objects with correctness rules

content.title: TLBs cache address translations, but software still owns ordering
content.body: The programmer view describes configurable instruction/data TLB entries and an optional shared TLB. The satp note warns that writing satp alone does not invalidate translation caches or order page-table updates.
content.caption: A TLB entry is a cached translation, not a permanent truth.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 Programmer View: RISC-V virtual memory
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMUEn and XLEN; CV32A6 supports Sv32, CV64A6 supports Sv39, and Bare mode is the default.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A6 CSRs: satp and SFENCE.VMA note
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Writing satp does not by itself order page-table updates or invalidate translation caches; SFENCE.VMA may be required.

content.speakerNotes: A TLB is a performance structure, but it has correctness consequences. The programmer view talks about configurable instruction and data TLB entries, and an optional shared second level. The CSR text gives the software warning: writing satp does not automatically order page-table updates or invalidate translation caches. That is exactly why SFENCE.VMA belongs in a serious memory lesson.

actions.text: A TLB is a performance structure, but it has correctness consequences. The programmer view talks about configurable instruction and data TLB entries, and an optional shared second level. The CSR text gives the software warning: writing satp does not automatically order page-table updates or invalidate translation caches. That is exactly why SFENCE.VMA belongs in a serious memory lesson.

questions.question: What is the safest first question before teaching CVA6 virtual memory?

options.label: Which CVA6 configuration and MMU mode are we discussing?
Is every CVA6 variant always Sv39?

questions.analysis: Virtual-memory behavior depends on configuration, XLEN, and MMUEn.

questions.question: What normally happens on a DTLB miss?

options.label: The frontend RAS pops an entry
The PTW performs a page-table walk and may update translation state or raise a fault
COMMIT_STAGE writes the data cache directly

questions.analysis: The page-table walker handles TLB misses and may produce translation or a page-fault exception.

questions.question: Which items are part of the virtual-memory mental model?

options.label: Branch target buffer replacement policy only

questions.analysis: BTB policy belongs to the frontend prediction unit, not the data-side translation path.

questions.question: What does the satp/SFENCE.VMA note teach?

options.label: Writing satp automatically orders every page-table update
Writing satp alone may not order page-table updates or invalidate translation caches
SFENCE.VMA is a data-cache replacement algorithm
satp is unrelated to address translation

questions.analysis: The CSR note explicitly warns that SFENCE.VMA may be required after page-table changes or ASID reuse.

sourceAnchors.claimSupported: CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMUEn and XLEN; CV32A6 supports Sv32, CV64A6 supports Sv39, and Bare mode is the default.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A6 CSRs: satp and SFENCE.VMA note
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Writing satp does not by itself order page-table updates or invalidate translation caches; SFENCE.VMA may be required.

content.speakerNotes: This quiz checks whether translation is a system, not just a diagram. Configuration comes first. TLB hit is the fast path. PTW handles misses. Page faults are a normal possible output. And software ordering around satp and SFENCE.VMA matters.

actions.text: This quiz checks whether translation is a system, not just a diagram. Configuration comes first. TLB hit is the fast path. PTW handles misses. Page faults are a normal possible output. And software ordering around satp and SFENCE.VMA matters.

content.title: Virtual memory changes what an address means
takeaways: CVA6 translation behavior depends on configuration, XLEN, and MMUEn.
TLB hits are fast; PTW handles misses and may raise page faults.
satp and SFENCE.VMA teach that translation caches need explicit ordering discipline.
content.nextStep: Next lesson: cache and interface boundaries, where D-cache, PMA, AXI/OBI, and SoC responsibilities must not be mixed.
content.closing: A virtual address is not a physical fact until translation says so.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 Programmer View: RISC-V virtual memory
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: CVA6 virtual memory depends on MMUEn and XLEN; CV32A6 supports Sv32, CV64A6 supports Sv39, and Bare mode is the default.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 deprecated design docs: MMU and PTW
sourceAnchors.locator: deprecated-design-docs/ex_stage.md Memory Management Unit / Page Table Walker
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The MMU contains ITLB, DTLB, and PTW; the PTW listens for ITLB or DTLB misses and raises page faults on error states.

content.speakerNotes: The takeaway is that address translation turns an address into a contract. Configuration decides whether translation exists. TLBs accelerate common cases. PTW handles misses. Faults and fences are part of correctness. Next we step outward to cache and interface boundaries, because the LSU does not live alone in the universe.

actions.text: The takeaway is that address translation turns an address into a contract. Configuration decides whether translation exists. TLBs accelerate common cases. PTW handles misses. Faults and fences are part of correctness. Next we step outward to cache and interface boundaries, because the LSU does not live alone in the universe.
